denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/28/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101235364          Group #: GRP-1156
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-125393
Claim #: CLM-2026-196269
Date of Service (requested): 02/08/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 02/10/2026 for Liothyronine (T3) adjunct therapy related to Hypothyroidism, inadequately controlled (E03.9). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Liothyronine (T3) adjunct therapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Hypothyroidism, inadequately controlled (E03.9)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 136 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG/MM_ series) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 136 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Hypothyroidism, inadequately controlled (E03.9) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101235364) and reference PA-2026-125393. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Hypothyroidism, inadequately controlled (E03.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Liothyronine (T3) adjunct therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. TSH 6.8 mIU/L on levothyroxine 150 mcg; persistent fatigue and weight gain. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that visit limit exceeded bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
